module_mood_contract: Joyful creation — 'it works, on my repo.' Student discovers the compound loop on a real bug from their own backlog; the CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence is unambiguously theirs. Any edit that makes M1 feel like a TDD tutorial or a tools-setup module steals the personal-authorship feeling that sets up M2's grounded-competence deepening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands at 8+ per phase and close.
Mood doesn't resolve the next module's teaching moment early.
Named teaching moment reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The compound step (Ex3: CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is forced inside the exercise's own structure. The Debrief block narrates this; the exercise file carries the prompt. A student cannot complete Ex3 without running the retro — the artifact is the evidence. Push-back moves for compound rubber-stamping are enumerated in the maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea honors the strategy doc's M1 mood contract and framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea ('The first compound loop closes on a trivial bug from your own backlog. Orient, fix tests-first without plan mode, retro into a personal CLAUDE.local.md born from the session, close the bug's ticket via one connector.') matches strategy doc M1 detail precisely — three exercises, same sequence, same artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: This file's close hands off to M2's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 43: 'Module 2 is where plan mode earns its keep: multi-file work, a second pass that pressure-tests the plan...' — matches M2's grounded-competence mood and plan-mode-at-depth arc. CLAUDE.local.md at the top of the next session is explicitly named as the M2 context-carry artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:19 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the training adds its own.' This is a cohort-management sentence in student-facing body prose. Rule 27 bans authored cohort-management beats (the diagnostic: is this sentence about the WORK or COHORT MANAGEMENT?). 'The room harvests' is cohort management. Not a full authored conversation, but it's a room-action embedded in body text.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut the sentence; the question stands without it. The trainer will run the harvest without a body cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module file contains no fenced prompt blocks — forcing functions live in the referenced exercise files (orient-and-introspect.md, fix-tests-first.md, compound-and-close.md). The module file's student-facing body describes the arc; it doesn't embed prose forcing functions. Architectural split is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block has Push-back moves, Plug points, Frameworks riffed on, and Meta (trainer) sections. No explicit leap-test row naming three observable Monday-morning outcomes with verifiable named verbs (e.g., 'opens /context on a repo they haven't touched yet', 'writes a test that proves the bug exists before touching the fix', 'ships a CLAUDE.local.md rule born from session evidence and edits at least one rule before saving'). The strategy doc names the M1 artifacts but the module-level maintainer block carries no standalone Leap test section.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a '## Leap test' row in the maintainer block with three named observable behaviors the engineer should exhibit on their own codebase by the next working day.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function's dominant failure mode and one recovery move documented.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The maintainer block's Push-back moves section covers all three exercises with named failure modes and Nerd/trainer escape hatches. Ex1: introspection-skip + /context-skipped + drifted-bug, each with a specific Nerd push. Ex2: tests-skipped + diff rubber-stamp. Ex3: rule rubber-stamp + team-vs-personal ambiguity + compound-summary confabulation + MCP install gate. The format is a named list rather than a formal table, but the diagnostic content is present and sufficient. Rule 47 requires 'one escape hatch per forcing function'; this block exceeds that bar.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — produced files listed as contract rows with stable path + producing prompt + consuming module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Rule 46 specifies 'Technical judge blocks ship on missing contract rows.' This is a technical-class finding. Story class notes the absence (./CLAUDE.local.md is named in Bridge as the M2 carry artifact but no formal artefact-contracts section exists in the maintainer block) and defers the blocking call to the technical judge.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points are integration commitments, not labels; UX — operator can paste each prompt without inventing setup.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plug points in maintainer block name concrete org-context kinds: 'Student's repo (chosen in prework) + bug Claude surfaced in prework + sponsor-stated ticket tracker.' These are integration commitments (the exercise prompts downstream depend on repo + bug + tracker being named). UX: module has no fenced prompts of its own; all prompts live in exercise files. A fast operator skipping body reads: Big Idea → exercises linked by name → Debrief summary → Bridge. No 'wait, what?' moments at module level. Exercise-level UX is out of scope for this judge.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; demo lectures (ceiling-showing) are openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 21: '[The wizard move]' — opener position. Strategy and module both describe this as a ceiling-showing demo (trainer demos a wizard-level move on a volunteer's codebase). Correct opener placement per rule 1. Line 40: '[How this training was built]' — placed after Debrief in closer position. This is a meta-frame lecture (naming a pattern the student just lived through). Correct closer placement.

M1 is not a contrast-mood module (it is joyful creation, not a contrast arc like M5). Rule 2 targets M5-style contrast-mood modules. One opener lecture (wizard move) before exercises is minimal. N/A for contrast-mood constraint; PASS for dosage check.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored — does not flatten or pre-resolve.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 63: 'Mood target: joyful creation — it works, on my repo.' Explicit. The module body stays in joyful-creation register throughout — no grounded-competence phrasing, no unease. Bridge teases M2's plan-mode discipline without delivering it. Contract honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 mood is joyful creation, not unease. Rule 2 is not triggered.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names positive disciplines (orient, fix, compound, close). Key Concepts name disciplines: 'Plan mode is overhead on a trivial bug', 'A CLAUDE.local.md born from how you actually worked reads different from one written from a blank page.' No failure-word titles.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — draft doesn't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No Skill use at M1 (per strategy doc: 'No Skill use at M1. First Skill use, first subagent, first authoring all land together at M3.'). Module correctly uses /context (slash command) and MCP (connector), not Skill invocation. Huryn's three-block frame is seeded without naming per strategy: 'Three-block memory — seeded in M1 without naming the three-block frame; the frame earns its name at M4 via recognition.' Module respects this — no three-block naming in student body. Plan mode restrained to Bridge/tease. All downstream moments intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — each bullet surfaces a theme instance.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts: 'Plan mode is overhead on a trivial bug' (theme: compounding — right tool for the scope); 'The agent can introspect on its own read; the self-report is a hypothesis, not ground truth. Assume 10% of what it says or does is misrepresentation' (themes: 90% correct + self-aware + grain of salt); '/context shows how much of your window has filled up' (theme: self-aware); 'A CLAUDE.local.md born from how you actually worked reads different from one written from a blank page' (theme: compounding + capture-your-world); 'The compound step lives in Exercise 3, after the PR ships' (theme: compounding); 'Personal rules vs. team rules is a split worth learning early' (theme: non-agentic-default / you make the decisions). Two or three themes surfaced per rule 5. Passes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs on 1-3 bullets, not a standalone themes section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets are in tactical module voice. No standalone '**Themes this module instances:**' section present. No breadcrumb bolding yet on any bullet — this is a style opportunity, not a violation. Rule 6 only bans the standalone themes section, which is absent.
